phase: 3 - Hypothesis and Testing (test minimally + backward trace to source)

experiment.name: per-key serialization probe (one variable)
experiment.change: In the test harness ONLY, wrap lookup()+reserve() in an asyncio.Lock keyed by the idempotency key (a dict of locks). Production code untouched. This is the single variable: does adding a per-key critical section across the lookup..reserve window eliminate the duplicate?

backward_trace: symptom: second psp_charge ch_B for ik_3af91c
<- IdempotencyStore.lookup(ik_3af91c) returned MISS (the gate let it through)
<- lookup() ran a plain SELECT; the row from request A's reserve() did not exist yet
<- reserve() (the INSERT) happens AFTER psp.charge(), and AFTER the other request already passed lookup()
ORIGIN: there is no atomic step that both checks AND claims the key. The 'bad value' is not data - it is the ABSENT mutual exclusion between lookup and reserve.
source_of_bug: app/payments/idempotency.py - lookup() and reserve() are a non-atomic check-then-act with the side effect in between; no per-key critical section.
note: The probe used an in-process lock only to PROVE causation. The real fix must not be an in-process lock (multiple workers/pods would each have their own) - it must be atomic at the shared store. That decision belongs to fix_design.
source: systematic-debugging Phase 3.2-3.3 'Test Minimally' + root-cause-tracing.md (trace backward to the original trigger, fix at source)
